name: CreateOrganizationUnitSupplier
auth_description: Requires a user of type `Employee` or `Api` with functionality `OrganizationUnitSuppliers:Create`

headers.name: EVA-User-Agent
headers.description: The user agent that is making these calls. Don't make this specific per device/browser but per application. This should be of the form: `MyFirstUserAgent/1.0.0`

headers.name: EVA-Requested-OrganizationUnitID
headers.description: The ID of the organization unit to run this request in.

headers.name: EVA-Requested-OrganizationUnit-Query
headers.description: The query that selects the organization unit to run this request in.

headers.name: EVA-IDs-Mode
headers.description: The IDs mode to run this request in. Currently only `ExternalIDs` is supported.

D.name: TimeOfDay
D.description: At what time of the day this should trigger

F.name: Day
F.description: The how-manieth day of the month. When a DayOfWeek = Monday is provided, this means 1st,2nd,etc.. monday. When DayOfWeek = null is provided, this meanst, 1st,2nd,etc.. day of the month.

F.name: DayOfWeek
F.description:
The day of the week, if set to `null` this means 'any day'
Possible values:

* `0` - Sunday
* `1` - Monday
* `2` - Tuesday
* `3` - Wednesday
* `4` - Thursday
* `5` - Friday
* `6` - Saturday

B.name: ExpandSetIntoIndividualOrganizationUnits
B.description: If set to true, this will be expanded into individual OrganizationUnit suppliers. Only works with `SupplierOrganizationUnitID` and `OrganizationUnitSetID`

B.name: OrganizationUnitID
B.description:
This will be the OrganizationUnit that will be supplied to by the supplier.

If neither OrganizationUnitID or OrganizationUnitSetID or OrganizationUnitIDs are specified then all children of the supplier are automatically supplied to.

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnit`

B.name: OrganizationUnitIDs
B.description:
These will be the OrganizationUnits that will be supplied to by the supplier.

If neither OrganizationUnitID or OrganizationUnitSetID or OrganizationUnitIDs are specified then all children of the supplier are automatically supplied to.

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnit`

B.name: OrganizationUnitSetID
B.description:
The OrganizationUnitSet that will be supplied to by the supplier. Set memberships are resolved dynamically: when the set's members change, the supplier relationships change with them.

Can be combined with any supplier-side parameter (single OU, OU set, or list of OUs). If neither OrganizationUnitID or OrganizationUnitSetID or OrganizationUnitIDs are specified then all children of the supplier are automatically supplied to (only valid when the supplier is a single OU).

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnitSet`

B.name: SchedulingConfig
B.description: Optional schedule that drives the daily refill of this relation's stock allocation rules. Evaluated in UTC. When omitted, the relation's rules are never refilled automatically (manual or config-triggered only). An `OrganizationUnitSupplier` `allocation_refilled` event export is emitted after each scheduled refill completes.

B.name: SupplierOrganizationUnitID
B.description:
This will be the OrganizationUnit that becomes the supplier in this supplier relationship.

Either SupplierOrganizationUnitID or SupplierOrganizationUnitSetID or SupplierOrganizationUnitIDs must be specified to be a valid request.

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnit`

B.name: SupplierOrganizationUnitIDs
B.description:
These will be the OrganizationUnits that becomes the suppliers in this supplier relationship.

Either SupplierOrganizationUnitID or SupplierOrganizationUnitSetID or SupplierOrganizationUnitIDs must be specified to be a valid request.

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnit`

B.name: SupplierOrganizationUnitSetID
B.description:
The OrganizationUnitSet that will act as the supplier. Set memberships are resolved dynamically: when the set's members change, the supplier relationships change with them.

Can be combined with any supplied-to parameter (single OU or OU set). When using a set as the supplier, a supplied-to OrganizationUnitID or OrganizationUnitSetID must be specified -- "supply all children" is not supported for set-based suppliers.

Either SupplierOrganizationUnitID or SupplierOrganizationUnitSetID or SupplierOrganizationUnitIDs must be specified to be a valid request.

This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnitSet`

B.name: Target
B.description:
Possible values:

* `0` - Default
* `1` - Replenishment
* `2` - SupplierReturns
* `3` - Pickup

K.name: Code
K.description: A unique ID identifying this error.

K.name: Message
K.description: A human-readable message describing the error.

K.name: RequestID
K.description: A unique ID identifying the request. Please provide this ID when contacting support.

K.name: Type
K.description: A short description of the error type.

L.name: ExternalIDs
L.description: When using the `ExternalIDs` mode, this will hold the resolved external IDs.

L.name: IsAsyncResultAvailable
L.description: This is a flag indicating whether the async request is done. If it is `null`, it is not an async service. If it is `false`, the response is not yet available. Do not process the properties in that case. It is is `true` the properties are filled with the result of the call.

L.name: UnresolvedExternalIDs
L.description: When using the `ExternalIDs` mode, this will hold the external IDs we couldn't resolve.

J.name: OrganizationUnitSupplierIDs
J.description: This is the ID of a `OrganizationUnitSupplier`
